text.title: Structured Output Patterns — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `structured-output-patterns` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: All structured calls go through StructuredOutputService — direct provider calls in business code are rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Centralisation makes metrics, failover, and schema evolution tractable; ad-hoc calls fork the behaviour.

rule.statement: Every call records (schema_name, provider, attempt, latency_ms, parse_success). Anonymous calls forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without per-schema metrics, the «50% of failures come from one schema» insight is invisible.

rule.statement: Maximum 1 retry-with-error; second failure falls back to alternate provider OR raises ParseFailure with full context.
rule.rationale: Unbounded retry masks underlying schema bugs; bounded retry + failover trades a bit of latency for production resilience.

rule.statement: Schema changes are additive-only OR explicitly version-bumped. Breaking renames require a new schema_v2 and migration window.
rule.rationale: In-place breaking changes silently break downstream consumers; additive evolution preserves backwards compatibility.

rule.statement: Streaming JSON is parsed incrementally with a partial-JSON parser (e.g., `partial-json-parser`). Don't wait for the closing brace.
rule.rationale: Buffering the whole stream defeats the streaming UX; incremental parse with `Optional[…]` Pydantic gives best of both.
